description: libcamera is the standard next-generation suite inherently replacing legacy raspistill bridging strict multi-threaded hardware-level camera ISP handling.

commands.explanation: Boots the optic sensor executing a quick 5-second graphical preview window explicitly validating that the physical ribbon connections and optics are responsive.
Extracts systemic configurations listing the exact specific underlying camera models (imx219, imx477) currently actively recognized upon the CSI ports.
Engages the sensor focusing arrays, compiles a standard uncompressed image feed, and encodes it executing an immediate JPG save.
Leverages the `still` command explicitly forcing the internal software encoder to preserve lossless clarity outputting to an absolute PNG schema.
Commands advanced optical arrays (like Pi Camera Module 3) triggering internal Voice Coil Motors to dial perfect sharp programmatic physical lens focus.
Forces the integrated system block to parse video capturing raw hardware-accelerated fluid H264 feeds for exactly the specific inputted milliseconds duration (e.g. 10s).
Establishes a limitless TCP stream fundamentally transmitting active raw internal video frames inherently broadcasting them live across the LAN network.
Initiates an automated timelapse execution shooting cyclical periodic absolute still frames precisely every targeted interval milliseconds natively scaling internal image numeration arrays.
Dynamically adjusts algorithmic shutter calculations inherently limiting trailing blur explicitly tuned for specific extreme lighting situations (normal, sport, night).
Absolutely circumvents all internal Post-Processing pipelines dumping pure uncompressed literal pixel Bayer arrays bypassing systemic JPG compression losses.
